definition: A PS2 homebrew launcher by krHACKen that starts PS1 games through Sony's PS2 POPS emulator. Rev13 does not include the emulator binaries.
Sony's PS2 PS1 emulator, also known in sources as SLBB-00001. Required binaries must be supplied legally by the user.
The POPStarter game image format produced from PS1 BIN/CUE sources by CUE2POPS. The extension should remain uppercase.
Virtual Memory Card files created per game folder, usually SLOT0.VMC and SLOT1.VMC.
Per-game text file used for raw cheat codes and POPStarter special directives such as $IGR5, $HDTVFIX, or $SMOOTH.
In-Game Reset, POPStarter's in-game exit/reset flow. It can show a menu or close POPS directly depending on $IGR commands.
POPStarter's exit-chain loader that can attempt to launch BOOT.ELF. Some BOOT.ELF targets black-screen, and PATCH_9.BIN can disable this path.
A modern driver-substitution project that gives exFAT USB support to closed-source homebrew that loads external USB drivers, including POPStarter.
A Lua/Enceladus-based standalone POPStarter launcher by El_isra, later forked for additional devices and HDD fixes.
A modified wLaunchELF/uLaunchELF build used to launch POPStarter VCDs without a per-game renamed ELF workflow.
The PS2 internal HDD partition/filesystem environment used by POPStarter HDD workflows.
Internal HDD partition/path for POPStarter VCD files. The dot and case matter.
Internal HDD common POPS folder used for files such as POPS.ELF, IOPRP252.IMG, POPSTARTER.ELF, and sometimes POPS_IOX.PAK.
SMB-mode prefix used when renaming POPSTARTER.ELF for a network launch, for example SB.Crash Bandicoot.ELF.
USB-mode prefix used by the classic renamed-ELF workflow, for example XX.Gran Turismo.ELF paired with Gran Turismo.VCD.
Legacy internal-HDD partition prefix for HDDOSD-visible POPStarter installs, where IMAGE0.VCD lives inside a PP.<game> partition.
Legacy hidden internal-HDD partition prefix for partition-installed POPStarter games, distinct from the modern shared __.POPS VCD location.
Additional USB POPStarter folders scanned after POPS. They can hold VCDs, but many support files must be copied into each folder rather than inherited from mass:/POPS.
KELF build of POPStarter used for HDDOSD / Sony Browser 2.00 style launch paths.
PS2 disc/partition metadata file. Legacy partition-installed POPStarter games can point BOOT2 at pfs:/IMAGE0.VCD.
PC utility used to convert PS1 BIN/CUE images into POPStarter .VCD images.
Older external PFS wrapper artifact that later became embedded in POPStarter's Beta 8 line.
POPStarter SMB memory-card config file containing PS2 IP address, subnet mask, and gateway/router IP on one line.
POPStarter SMB memory-card config file containing SMB host IP/share name, with optional username and password lines.
OPL Apps SMB path prefix reportedly required by some later OPL betas instead of smb: for conf_apps.cfg entries.
OPL Apps metadata file placed beside an app ELF. For these workflows it normally contains a visible title line and a boot= line matching the ELF filename.
BDM/BDM Assault mode used by modern workflows to support non-HDD devices such as USB exFAT, MX4SIO, or MMCE through replacement device drivers.
External POPStarter patch file family. Individual numbers map to different behaviors; the full map still requires package/archive verification.
External fix bundle family mentioned in seed notes and Hugopocked-related workflows. Full mapping remains missing.
Game-specific compatibility fixes discussed on PSX-Place, placed in the per-game folder matching the VCD basename.
